name: Hank Williams
title: Last known photo of Hank Williams alive, taken on December 28, 1952, at the Elite Cafe in Montgomery, Alabama, four days before he died en route to a New Year's Day show in Ohio.
description: This is the last known photograph of Hank Williams alive, an informal snapshot taken by an unidentified guest at a private musicians' union party at the Elite Cafe in Montgomery, Alabama, on December 28, 1952. Williams appears without his trademark cowboy hat. Four days later, on January 1, 1953, his driver Charles Carr stopped for gas at the Burdette's Pure Oil station in Oak Hill, West Virginia, while ferrying Williams overnight from Knoxville to a scheduled New Year's Day concert in Canton, Ohio. When Carr returned to the car, he found Williams unresponsive in the back seat. The coroner ruled the cause of death as heart failure, with chloral hydrate, alcohol, and morphine listed as contributing factors. Williams was 29 years old.

lyrics.text:
Did you ever see a robin weep
When leaves begin to die?
Like me, he's lost the will to live
I'm so lonesome I could cry

bio: Hiram King “Hank” Williams was an American singer, songwriter, and musician, and one of the most significant and influential musicians of the 20th century. An early pioneer of country music, his catalog of standards includes “I'm So Lonesome I Could Cry,” “Hey, Good Lookin',” “Your Cheatin' Heart,” and “I Saw the Light.” He was inducted into the inaugural class of the Country Music Hall of Fame in 1961.

location.name: Elite Cafe, 121 Montgomery Street, Montgomery, AL 36104
